name: Remembrance Trail
name_original: Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular por Riaño
description: Enjoy an easy 3.6-mile circular hike by Riaño, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding mountain ranges in just under two hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Riaño, nestled within the Cantabrian Mountains, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by the expansive Riaño Reservoir, often referred to as the "Leonese Fjords," and dramatic limestone peaks. These mountains provide a rugged backdrop, with elevations reaching up to 1,679 meters at Pico Gilbo. The area features a mix of steep mountain terrain, reservoir-side paths, and routes through the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. Best hiking trails around Riaño The most popular hiking route is Pico Gilbo Loop Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de la Mitología Leonesa - circular from Carande through the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This circular route explores the Regional Park, featuring varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Remembrance Trail, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail leading along the Riaño Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around Riaño is defined by the dramatic "Leonese Fjords," the rugged Cantabrian Mountains, and the expansive Riaño reservoir. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Riaño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Riaño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Riaño?
A: Riaño, situated within the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities. There are nearly 40 official hiking trails available, catering to all skill levels from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Riaño?
A: Yes, Riaño has several easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050055715/senda-de-la-mitologia-leonesa-circular-from-carande-through-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Mitología Leonesa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km circular route through the Regional Park. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027246786/remembrance-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remembrance Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.9 km path along the Riaño Reservoir, featuring informational panels about the submerged villages.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Riaño?
A: Riaño is famous for its dramatic 'Leonese Fjords' and the expansive Riaño Reservoir, surrounded by the rugged Cantabrian Mountains. Many trails offer panoramic vistas of these features. Iconic viewpoints like Mirador Alto Valcayo and Mirador Las Hazas provide stunning perspectives, especially of Pico Gilbo, often called the 'Leonese Matterhorn', and the turquoise waters of the reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Riaño?
A: Yes, Riaño offers several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050055715/senda-de-la-mitologia-leonesa-circular-from-carande-through-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Mitología Leonesa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular trail. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384023193/mirador-pena-salguera-circular-desde-riano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Peña Salguera — circular desde Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, which offers great views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050063229/valle-de-salio-circular-from-carande-through-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Salio&lt;/a&gt; also provides a moderate circular experience through the Regional Park.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Riaño?
A: Beyond the stunning natural scenery, Riaño offers several points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing&lt;/a&gt; at Mirador de las Hazas for a unique photo opportunity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are also highly recommended. For those interested in infrastructure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Riaño?
A: The hiking trails in Riaño are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic 'Leonese Fjords' scenery, the diverse terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for all abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Riaño?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050074263/pico-gilbo-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This difficult 8 km route involves a demanding climb to Pico Gilbo (1,679 meters), rewarding trekkers with unparalleled panoramic views of the Riaño reservoir and the surrounding Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Can I observe wildlife while hiking in Riaño?
A: Yes, the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park is rich in wildlife. Hiking in areas like the Anciles Valley offers opportunities to spot bison. The region is also home to goats, chamois, wild boar, and various raptors like eagles and vultures. Boat trips on the reservoir can also provide chances to see some of these animals.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riaño?
A: Riaño offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with fewer crowds. Summer is popular for its warm weather, though some higher trails can be challenging in the heat. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but requires appropriate gear and awareness of snow conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there any historical routes or paths to explore in the Riaño area?
A: Yes, Riaño is steeped in history, offering routes that trace ancient paths. You can explore sections of the GR-1 Sendero Histórico, which winds through rural Spain's mountainous landscapes. Other historic routes in the area include 'The Forgotten Way,' 'The Vadinian Route,' and 'The Lebaniego Way,' providing a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What types of terrain will I encounter on the trails in Riaño?
A: The terrain in Riaño is quite varied. You'll find everything from gentle, flat paths along the reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027246786/remembrance-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remembrance Trail&lt;/a&gt;, to more rugged and steep ascents in the Cantabrian Mountains, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050074263/pico-gilbo-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes traverse through forests, open meadows, and along the dramatic 'Leonese Fjords,' offering a diverse hiking experience.
tips: The views that you will have from this viewpoint seem to have come out of a fairy tale. Wonderful panoramic views of the entire Riaño reservoir, the Gilbo peak behind and the entire landscape of the Leonese fjords, worthy of a postcard.

Also known as the Las Hazas viewpoint, here is the largest swing in Spain and offers incredible views of the town, the reservoir and the beautiful "fjords of Leon". A place worth visiting to enjoy the wonderful landscape of this area. .

Originally built in order to preserve food by keeping it away from humidity and animals, nowadays they have ceased to serve this function but have become very colorful and picturesque monuments that somehow portray and allow us to understand the history and culture of the place.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular by Riaño?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is relatively short and easy. It typically takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete, making it perfect for a leisurely stroll.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! The Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular by Riaño is categorized as an easy hike. It features easily accessible paths and is suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels, including families with children.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route by Riaño, implying it starts and ends near the town. While specific parking isn't detailed, Riaño is a popular starting point for various routes, so you'll likely find parking options within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route features easily accessible paths. While the broader regional park has abrupt relief, this specific trail is designed for a less strenuous experience, offering pleasant walking conditions.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this circular route, you can enjoy several notable points of interest. Keep an eye out for the Valcayo Viewpoint, the unique Riaño Swing, and the historic Hórreo of Salio - Riaño. You'll also get to experience the beautiful natural environment surrounding Riaño, including views of the reservoir and the dramatic mountain backdrop.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino Vadiniense, the Paseo del Recuerdo, and the Travesía de Valcayo 1, as well as passing through the Plaza de Cimadevilla.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular by Riaño dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the regional park generally welcomes dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park is beautiful year-round. The extensive beech forests display changing colors with the seasons, making autumn particularly scenic. Spring and summer offer pleasant weather for hiking, while winter can provide a different, snowy landscape, though conditions may vary.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the regional park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access hiking trails within the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the regional park?
faqs.answer: The Montaña de Riaño is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot emblematic species such as chamois, roe deer, and various birds of prey like griffon vultures. The park is also home to brown bears and Iberian wolves, though sightings are rare.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trail in Riaño?
faqs.answer: Riaño is a picturesque town and a popular starting point for routes. You can expect to find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town, perfect for a refreshment before or after your hike.
